item.title: Talks set to begin in Islamabad as U.S. and Iran seek path to end war
item.description: Both Iran and the U.S. traded allegations of ceasefire violations ahead of talks set to begin Saturday with Pakistan as the host and Israel not in attendance.

item.title: Parrot goes on underwater adventure in his custom-built submarine
item.description: “He enjoyed it,” said the owner of Bebe the parrot, who has also come along for skydiving, skiing and 500-mile bike rides.

item.title: U.S. team heads to Iran peace talks with a gulf separating the two sides
item.description: Amid allegations of bad faith and ceasefire violations from both Washington and Tehran, negotiators travel to Islamabad for high-stakes talks.

item.title: U.S. and Iran want to end the war, but peace talks hinge on these issues
item.description: The sides are far apart on Iran’s nuclear program, the Strait of Hormuz and the Israeli campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: U.S. negotiators to ask Iran to release detained Americans
item.description: The request for the Americans’ release may be delayed if the talks between President Donald Trump’s team and Iran prove difficult, according to people briefed on the plans.

item.title: Iran ceasefire under pressure as strikes in Lebanon continue ahead of talks
item.description: President Donald Trump accused Iran of “doing a very poor job” of opening the Strait of Hormuz to oil traffic, as shipping flows remained effectively blocked.

item.title: China helped broker Iran truce, but it’s wary of deeper involvement
item.description: Beijing urged Iran to accept a ceasefire, but it hasn’t agreed to act as a guarantor of the deal in America’s messy war.

item.title: Israel to open direct talks with Lebanon but not halt attacks on Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that his country was ready for direct negotiations amid international calls for Israel to stop its bombing in Lebanon.

item.title: First major Atlantic hurricane season outlook calls for below-average activity
item.description: A developing “super El Niño” may reduce the number of storms in the Atlantic.

item.title: Tako? Takou? TACO-ru? Decoding the Trump TACO meme around the world.
item.description: A Washington Post reporter’s doctor in Seoul had a question — how to translate “chicken out,” as in “Trump always chickens out,” into Korean?

item.title: In war-torn Lebanon, migrants and LGBTQ+ people face hard path to safety
item.description: Those who are able head for second homes, move in with family or stay in hotels. Those who aren’t crowd into cramped shelters, stadiums or parking lots, or worse.

item.title: Want to be a Canadian? It’s never been easier.
item.description: Canada tweaks its dual-citizenship rule, allowing far more to apply.

item.title: After Trump pauses war, Iranians fly flags of victory, not surrender
item.description: Amid fierce disagreements, the dramatic, last-minute decision to halt attacks seems less like an exit ramp than a rest stop for all sides.

item.title: Trump to discuss leaving NATO in meeting with alliance’s leader, White House says
item.description: The president, long a NATO skeptic, has been especially angry at alliance members in recent weeks for declining to take part in the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Hegseth, Caine strike different tones on Iran ceasefire
item.description: The defense secretary described the state of hostilities mostly in past tense. The Joint Chiefs chairman took a more cautious approach.

item.title: Israel kills dozens in Beirut, claims Lebanon is not part of Iran truce
item.description: Israeli strikes pounded the Lebanese capital, killing dozens of people in an aerial barrage hours after the U.S.-Iran ceasefire took hold.

item.title: Halt in strikes appears to hold after Trump, Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire
item.description: With Washington and Tehran each claiming victory, the agreement raised the prospect of some respite after nearly six weeks of bombing.

item.title: Trump administration expected to slash Iran war funding request
item.description: The eventual ask of Congress is likely to fall between $80 billion and $100 billion, officials said, less than half the amount of an earlier proposal to offset costs of the conflict.

item.title: After pager attack on Hezbollah, Hungary offered help to Iran
item.description: Revelations about a 2024 call offering assistance raise questions about Hungary’s ties to Iran as the Trump administration backs Prime Minister Viktor Orban for reelection.

item.title: Hero rat who sniffed out over 100 land mines is honored with giant statue
item.description: “Magawa was one of the best rats we’ve ever had,” said Michael Raine, who works for Apopo, a nonprofit that trains animals to detect land mines.

item.title: Pope Leo denounces Trump’s threat to destroy Iran’s ‘whole civilization’
item.description: The Chicago-born pope suggested fellow Americans call their congressional representatives and ask for peace, not war.

item.title: U.S. journalist Shelly Kittleson, kidnapped in Iraq, is freed in prisoner swap
item.description: Kittleson, a freelancer for several U.S. outlets, was seized last week in Baghdad by Kataib Hezbollah, a Shiite militia aligned with Iran.

item.title: JD Vance campaigns for far-right nationalist Viktor Orban in Hungary
item.description: The vice president traveled to Budapest as Trump’s deadline for an Iran deal loomed Tuesday, backing the administration’s closest ideological ally in Europe.

item.title: Hegseth’s rosy claims about Iran war contradict reality, officials say
item.description: The defense secretary’s rosy portrayal of U.S. success in the conflict risks misinforming the public and the president, observers worry.

item.title: As Trump escalates war, Iranian civilians fear they will pay the price
item.description: People in Iran say they could be left to pick up the pieces if President Donald Trump destroys the country’s infrastructure and economy.

item.title: With threat to destroy Iran’s ‘civilization,’ Trump fuels war crime fears
item.description: President Donald Trump said the United States would target “every” Iranian bridge and power plant. Experts say such blanket action violates international law.

item.title: Trump warns ‘a whole civilization will die’ if Iran doesn’t make a deal
item.description: The president had issued a deadline of 8 p.m. Eastern time for Iran to open up the Strait of Hormuz, pledging destruction by midnight if leaders don’t comply.

item.title: Ramen, K-beauty, clothes face shortages as Iran war hits Asia’s supplies
item.description: Even potato chips aren’t safe from the ripple effects of President Trump’s Iran war, which is disrupting supply chains across Asia.

item.title: Trump claims ‘active’ peace talks with Iran as bombing deadline approaches
item.description: The president has given Iranian officials until 8 p.m. Tuesday to make a ceasefire deal or face widespread destruction. Tehran on Monday reasserted its rejection of U.S. demands.

item.title: Kidnapped U.S. journalist believed alive in militia’s Iraqi stronghold
item.description: U.S. and Iraqi officials believe freelancer Shelly Kittleson was kidnapped last week by Kataib Hezbollah, a paramilitary group with links to Iran.

item.title: Trump to describe risky rescue mission as Iran rebuffs U.S. war demands
item.description: The rescue of an F-15 crewman averted a potentially disastrous POW situation, but Trump’s threats showed rising frustration amid an increasingly dangerous and politically unpopular war.

item.title: Trump administration’s secrecy on health deals alarms experts, governments
item.description: A dearth of information has been disclosed about the agreements, fueling speculation that the “America First” approach to foreign aid is exploitative.

item.title: War-driven energy crisis boosts China’s sales pitch for renewable tech
item.description: Sales of Chinese electric vehicles and solar panels have surged since the start of the Iran war, companies say.

item.title: From Oman, a waterfront view of the embattled Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Residents of Khasab, a sleepy exclave that depends on fishing and tourism, are frustrated by the war in Iran and fearful of what’s next.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran with ‘Hell’ over Strait of Hormuz in profane post
item.description: Trump escalated threats against Iran’s power plants, bridges and other infrastructure in an expletive-laden post on Truth Social on Easter morning.

item.title: It’s Holy Week, but Jerusalem’s Old City is quiet and eerily empty
item.description: The Austrian Hospice urges groups of Christian pilgrims to book 16 months ahead. One night this week, a receptionist warned a Post reporter she would be the only guest.

item.title: On Easter, Pope Leo delivers commanding message of peace to a world at war
item.description: “Let those who have weapons lay them down!” the first American pope declared. The White House’s war in Iran and nativist agenda at home is testing the Vatican.

item.title: Vance heads to Hungary as MAGA ally Orban trails in polls and election nears
item.description: Viktor Orban, who has built strong ties to the MAGA movement, faces a tough electoral challenge from center-right candidate Peter Magyar on April 12.

item.title: American universities in Middle East brace for Iranian retaliation
item.description: After the U.S.-Israeli campaign struck Iranian universities, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps called American schools in the Middle East “legitimate targets.”

item.title: Search for missing U.S. F-15 crew member underway in Iran
item.description: Iranian state media said “many people” are searching for the missing airman after an F-15 fighter jet and an A-10 attack plane were lost to hostile fire.

item.title: Chinese firms market Iran war intelligence ‘exposing’ U.S. forces
item.description: Private Chinese technology companies — some with ties to the military — are marketing detailed intelligence on movements of U.S. forces in Iran, even as Beijing seeks to keep its distance.

item.title: Epstein victims demand meeting with King Charles in Washington
item.description: A state visit intended to mark 250 years of U.S. independence could become a test of the British monarchy’s willingness to confront one of its most difficult controversies.

item.title: Russian attacks on Ukraine kill 5 people; nearly 20 animals die in fire
item.description: Ukraine’s Emergency Services posted images of rescue workers trying to save the animals — in one instance administering CPR to a dog — at the clinic in Chabany.

item.title: U.S. fighter jet crashes in Iran; search launched for 2 crew members
item.description: It’s the first known loss of an American aircraft inside Iranian territory since the war began a month ago.

item.title: As U.S. bombs Iran, Pope Leo criticizes the idea of a ‘God of war’
item.description: Some Trump administration officials cloak the war on Iran in religious terms, but the Chicago-born pope said that God “does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war.”

item.title: Trump heralds regime change, but Iran’s leaders remain hard-liners
item.description: The president says Iran’s remaining leaders are more reasonable. But assassinations have left in place an emboldened government driving a hard bargain, officials say.

item.title: Cuba says it will release over 2,000 prisoners, as U.S. keeps up pressure
item.description: The move, described by the communist government as a “humanitarian and sovereign gesture,” comes as the U.S. enforces a crippling oil blockade on the island.

item.title: Sudanese rebels used rape as a weapon, aid group says
item.description: Sexual violence is a widespread weapon of war in Sudan. “Survivors frequently and clearly identified the perpetrators as RSF fighters,” Doctors Without Borders says.

item.title: Israelis celebrate Passover in a parking garage turned bomb shelter
item.description: People broke matzoh, read from the Haggadah and sang — with no need to worry about when the next air raid siren might sound.

item.title: Nations to meet on Strait of Hormuz as Trump calls for others to ‘take the lead’
item.description: The president has said responsibility for reopening the waterway should fall largely to European and Asian countries that depend on it more than the U.S. does for oil.

item.title: Migrant workers keep Dubai running but can’t afford to flee Iranian strikes
item.description: As Iran’s retaliatory attacks hit regional centers of commerce, such as Dubai, the majority of the deaths have been among migrant workers who could not afford to flee.

item.title: Iran war’s fuel crisis spurs panic, violence in nations facing shortages
item.description: Gasoline is being stolen and gas station workers in Bangladesh, Pakistan and India have been killed over fuel shortages and soaring prices caused by the war against Iran.

item.title: Risky commando plan to seize Iran’s uranium came at Trump’s request
item.description: A plan briefed to Trump last week to insert ground forces to remove Iran’s nuclear material would be a very difficult endeavor of a type never before attempted in wartime.

item.title: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
item.description: Iraq’s interior ministry said it had arrested one suspect, seized a car and was looking for accomplices.

item.title: Trump slams allies after Italy blocks U.S. use of air base for Iran war
item.description: European allies are continuing their resistance to the war against Iran despite President Donald Trump’s exasperation and threats of backing away from NATO.

item.title: Israel mandates death penalty for West Bank Palestinians who kill in terrorist acts
item.description: Opposition lawmakers, rights advocates and some foreign governments condemned the law as discriminatory. Israelis in the territory are tried in different courts.

item.title: Trump says U.S. negotiating with Iranian leadership, despite denials
item.description: Iran denied Trump’s claims that direct talks with more a moderate regime in Tehran were making “great progress.”

item.title: Thieves steal works by Cézanne, Renoir, Matisse in less than 3 minutes
item.description: Four masked men are believed to have forced their way through an entry gate, grabbed the paintings and escaped by climbing a fence, Italy’s Carabinieri said.

item.title: Israel suspends battalion that detained CNN crew in West Bank
item.description: Reservists from the IDF’s ultra-Orthodox Netzah Yehuda unit held CNN journalists for two hours on Thursday.

item.title: Outrage grows over Israeli restrictions to Jerusalem sites during Holy Week
item.description: The move by Israeli police to block Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa from the holiest site in Christianity was reversed after drawing criticism

item.title: In allied campaign, one war mission fell to Israel — killing top Iranians
item.description: An assassination apparatus that Israel spent decades building has been transformed in recent years to include more spies and a new AI platform, officials said.

item.title: U.S. will let Russian oil tanker reach Cuba, breaking Trump’s effective fuel blockade
item.description: The Trump administration had threatened to punish countries who attempted to break his effective fuel blockade, aimed at weakening the communist government.

item.title: Pentagon prepares for weeks of ground operations in Iran
item.description: If President Trump approves the plans, such an effort would mark a new phase of the war that could be significantly more dangerous to U.S. troops than the first four weeks.

item.title: Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen launch attack on Israel for first time in war
item.description: The missile attack by the Houthis in Yemen marks an escalation of the war in the Middle East and may pose further risks to shipping in the region.

item.title: Dread deepens among U.S. allies in Asia over a protracted Mideast war
item.description: Japan, Taiwan and other Asian countries that rely on the U.S. for security worry that the Iran war is drawing American military assets and focus away from containing China.

item.title: Iran’s attacks on Gulf neighbors cut off food and medicine for millions
item.description: The war has disrupted shipping routes, raised fuel and insurance prices, closed airports, and left aid groups with tons of essential supplies stuck in warehouses.

item.title: At least 10 U.S. troops wounded in Iranian attack on Saudi air base
item.description: The strike on Prince Sultan Air Base also damaged at least two Air Force refueling aircraft and underscored that despite weeks of strikes, Iran still poses a threat.

item.title: Nearly 1,500 Iranian civilians killed in U.S., Israeli strikes, report says
item.description: The findings appear to be the most comprehensive estimates yet of the growing toll of dead and wounded civilians in the month-long war.

item.title: U.S. uses hundreds of Tomahawk missiles on Iran, alarming some at Pentagon
item.description: More than 850 Tomahawks have been fired in just four weeks, people familiar with the matter said, alarming some Pentagon officials because the weapon’s supply is limited.

item.title: Cuba looks to Vatican for help as U.S. pressure plunges island into crisis
item.description: Cuban officials have petitioned Pope Leo XIV to help persuade the Trump administration to ease its oil embargo, which is causing crippling fuel shortages and blackouts.

item.title: $500 tee times, private yachts: Bachelor(ette) trips are out of control
item.description: Bachelor and bachelorette trips have become increasingly elaborate and costly, with social media and influencer culture driving the trend.

item.title: Far fewer immigrants are moving to big cities in U.S., data shows
item.description: New York, Los Angeles and Chicago are among the metro areas seeing steep declines in net immigration amid the Trump administration’s crackdown.

item.title: Israel says it killed Iran’s top naval commander in bid to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Israel said Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps’ navy, was responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian authorities did not immediately comment.

item.title: Pentagon considers diverting Ukraine military aid to the Middle East
item.description: A shift would highlight the growing trade-offs required for the U.S. to sustain its war with Iran as the conflict depletes the military’s critical munitions.

item.title: Gulf countries want Trump to end Iran war — but not yet, officials say
item.description: The Saudis and Emiratis fear a deal that leaves the region less stable, and they have indicated support for an escalated campaign to force concessions from Tehran.

item.title: China bars executives at Meta-owned AI company from leaving country
item.description: Manus’s CEO and chief scientist are facing scrutiny from Beijing over the company’s $2 billion sale to Meta.

item.title: Iraq accuses U.S. of killing 7 soldiers in clinic strike
item.description: As Iraq claimed “heinous aggression,” the U.S. denied it targeted a clinic. The incident threatens to sour relations between the countries amid the Iran war.

item.title: Russia hits Ukrainian cities, intensifying bombing as U.S. focuses on Iran
item.description: Ukraine said eight people were killed as Russia launched a bombardment that included its largest single-day drone assault of the war.

item.title: Pakistan’s effort to mediate U.S.-Iran talks appears to gather steam
item.description: Iran praised Pakistan’s “good intentions” but did not say how it had responded. A Pakistani analyst described “significant and rapid progress” in indirect talks.

item.title: Iran’s missiles pierce Israel’s defenses, raising doubts about interceptors
item.description: Concern that Iran was amassing missiles to overwhelm defenses was a key factor in the push for war, officials said, and recent strikes laid bare Israel’s vulnerability.

item.title: Army paratroopers ordered to Middle East as U.S. weighs next Iran move
item.description: The Pentagon on Tuesday ordered a couple thousand paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division to deploy to the Middle East.

item.title: Audio from cockpit recorder reveals error before deadly LaGuardia crash
item.description: LaGuardia’s air traffic control tower failed to recognize it had granted permission for a plane and an emergency vehicle to use the same runway, officials said.

item.title: Justices seem inclined to revive Trump policy blocking migrants at border
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed a policy that would allow agents at U.S. borders to block migrants from entering the country to seek asylum.

item.title: Rare Middle East storm could bring floods, damaging winds and tornadoes
item.description: Parts of the Arabian Peninsula and Persian Gulf could be hit by strong thunderstorms later this week. Major highways and airports in the region could be inundated.

item.title: Trump says peace talks progressing as Iran officials deny negotiations
item.description: Egypt, Pakistan and Turkey have taken the lead in efforts to broker a peace deal between the United States and Iran, though conversations have been indirect thus far.

item.title: Israel and Iran exchange strikes as Trump says U.S. is negotiating end to war
item.description: Israel and three Gulf states reported Iranian attacks a day after President Donald Trump said the U.S. would pause strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure during talks.

item.title: He was Rubio’s friend and roommate. Now Rubio will testify against him.
item.description: Former congressman David Rivera, accused of secretly lobbying for Nicolás Maduro’s government in Venezuela, climbed Miami politics alongside Marco Rubio.

item.title: Trump says U.S. is negotiating end to war with Iran
item.description: Trump said he had told the U.S. military to postpone strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, while talks proceed.

item.title: Ambulances set ablaze at London synagogue in attack Starmer calls antisemitic
item.description: No one was hurt in the blaze, which damaged four ambulances operated by a Jewish charity in north London’s Golders Green neighborhood, officials said.

item.title: At least one winner emerges from Iran war: U.S. natural gas exporters
item.description: President Donald Trump’s “energy dominance” agenda gets a boost as governments in Asia scramble to substitute Middle East fuel.

item.title: Trump threats, U.S. troop build-up raise specter of battle for Hormuz
item.description: Israelis said securing the strait for energy shipments could become the war’s main goal now that regime change and ending Iran’s ability to obtain a nuclear weapon seem out of reach.

item.title: Joe Kent says he wants Trump to hear MAGA opposition to Iran war
item.description: In an interview with The Post after resigning from the administration in protest, the veteran said he is taking his message to the president’s supporters.

item.title: Iran refuses to give in, seeking greater global economic pain
item.description: Tehran’s unwillingness to capitulate as the war enters its fourth week is wrapped up in the power it exerts over the Strait of Hormuz, officials in the region say.

item.title: When my friend came home from Maduro’s torture prison
item.description: The Washington Post’s Venezuela reporter writes on a couple divided by the authoritarian regime in Caracas — and their joyful reunion.

item.title: IAEA looking into Iran’s report that Natanz nuclear site hit in strikes
item.description: Human rights organizations say they fear Iran will carry out mass executions after the public hangings this week of three men who took place in anti-government protests.

item.title: BTS thrills K-pop fans with free comeback show for thousands in Seoul
item.description: The South Korean megastars thrilled tens of thousands of fans with their first concert in nearly four years following a break for military service and solo careers.

item.title: The Middle East celebrates Eid and Nowruz under the shadow of war
item.description: Missile exchanges have jarred with the spirit of the holidays underway across the region.

item.title: Russian ship potentially bringing oil to Cuba is test of Kremlin allyship
item.description: A Kremlin spokesman said the Russian government is discussing “possible options for assisting Cuba in the difficult situation it finds itself in.”

item.title: To tilt Hungarian election, Russians proposed staging assassination attempt
item.description: To aid Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a friend of Russia, in his election, operatives proposed “the Gamechanger” — a staged assassination attempt to stir supporters.

item.title: Trump administration lifts sanctions on millions of barrels of Iranian oil
item.description: The Treasury Department’s authorization of the sale of Iranian crude already at sea is likely to provide revenue for Iran’s war effort against the U.S.

item.title: Cuba rejects U.S. Embassy request to bring in fuel, calls it ‘shameless’
item.description: An effective U.S. oil blockade has worsened the island’s energy crisis. The U.S. Embassy in Havana, too, is feeling the effects.

item.title: 4 women killed in West Bank are first Palestinian deaths in Iran war
item.description: They were at a salon getting their nails done for Eid al-Fitr. It was unclear who fired the missile.

item.title: Hezbollah is bombing northern Israel again. Residents are staying put.
item.description: Rather than evacuation, Israel’s defense minister said the mission now is “to protect northern communities against raids and anti-tank fire.”

item.title: Deepening Iran conflict exposes cracks in U.S. and Israeli objectives
item.description: Israel’s assault on the world’s largest natural gas deposit has renewed tensions between the two allies’ end goals in Iran.

item.title: U.S. mulls lifting oil sanctions on Iran as it hits key Gulf energy sites
item.description: The price of Brent crude briefly climbed past $117 per barrel as Qatar, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait reported more attacks on infrastructure and the U.S. and Iran traded threats.

item.title: Economic fallout from U.S.-led war is hitting the rest of the world harder
item.description: Because of greater exposure to disruptions in the Middle East, such as high natural gas prices, businesses and consumers outside the U.S. are being hit harder by the war in Iran.

item.title: Pakistani strike killed hundreds, Afghanistan says, as regional conflicts boil
item.description: As attention focuses on Iran, the conflict between two of its neighbors is escalating.

item.title: Israel steps up assassinations as Iran war widens to energy facilities
item.description: The conflict continues to roil global energy markets. On Wednesday, an attack on Iran’s South Pars gas field sent energy prices soaring.

item.title: Gabbard tells senators Iranian regime is degraded but still intact
item.description: The director of national intelligence provided the Senate Intelligence Committee with mixed messages about the state of Iran’s nuclear program before the war began.

item.title: Rubio says Cuba’s efforts to improve its economy are failing
item.description: Cuba “can’t fix” its economy, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said, as President Donald Trump said his administration will be “doing something with Cuba very soon.”

item.title: Japan’s leader heads to D.C. bearing gifts of peace for a president at war
item.description: Japan’s super-popular prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, will visit the White House on Thursday, as President Donald Trump looks to allies for military help against Iran.

item.title: This Emirati billionaire put a voice to Gulf anger over Trump’s war in Iran
item.description: In a post on X, the hotel magnate lambasted the president for not considering collateral damage, although he later told The Post: “I blame Trump, but I blame the Iranians more.”

item.title: Hezbollah ignites blowback from its base as over 1 million flee war with Israel
item.description: The militant group’s attacks on Israel have sparked anger even among its most loyal Shiite supporters in Lebanon, weakening its clout as the war widens.

item.title: Israel says it killed Iran’s top security official, along with paramilitary boss
item.description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s lead security official, and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani were “eliminated,” Israel said. Iran has not commented on the strikes.

item.title: European leaders rebuff Trump’s call to open the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump has expressed frustration over a lack of military assistance from allies, but European leaders are reluctant to join a conflict he started without consulting them.

item.title: U.S. tells all its embassies to ‘immediately’ review security after strikes
item.description: The State Department order to review security at “ALL posts worldwide” follows persistent attacks on U.S. embassies, with almost 300 reported in Iraq alone.

item.title: Senior official openly breaks with White House, resigns over war
item.description: Joe Kent, a close aide to Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, cited deliberate Israeli “misinformation” and lies to President Donald Trump about a “swift path to victory.”
